Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava

Class DefaultGuavaRequirements

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava.DefaultGuavaRequirements
All Implemented Interfaces:
GuavaRequirements, Configuration
public final class DefaultGuavaRequirements extends Object implements GuavaRequirements
Default implementation of GuavaRequirements.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DefaultGuavaRequirements()
    Creates a default implementation of GuavaRequirements.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    assertionsAreEnabled()
    Indicates if assertThat() should delegate to requireThat(); otherwise, it won't do anything.
    <K, V> MultimapVerifier<K,V>
    assertThat(Multimap<K,V> actual, String name)
    Same as GuavaRequirements.requireThat(Multimap, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    GuavaRequirements
    copy()
    Returns a copy of this configuration.
    Map<String,Object>
    getContext()
    Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.
    boolean
    isCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates if stack trace references to this library should be removed.
    boolean
    isDiffEnabled()
    Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <K, V> MultimapVerifier<K,V>
    requireThat(Multimap<K,V> actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
    String
    toString(Object value)
    Returns the String representation of an object.
    <K, V> MultimapValidator<K,V>
    validateThat(Multimap<K,V> actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
    GuavaRequirements
    withAssertionsDisabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
    GuavaRequirements
    withAssertionsEnabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
    GuavaRequirements
    withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be removed.
    GuavaRequirements
    withConfiguration(Configuration configuration)
    Replaces a verifier's configuration.
    GuavaRequirements
    withContext(String name, Object value)
    Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message.
    GuavaRequirements
    withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    GuavaRequirements
    withoutAnyContext()
    Removes all contextual information associated with the exception message.
    GuavaRequirements
    withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that stack trace references to this library should be kept.
    GuavaRequirements
    withoutContext(String name)
    Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
    GuavaRequirements
    withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <T> GuavaRequirements
    withoutStringConverter(Class<T> type)
    Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
    <T> GuavaRequirements
    withStringConverter(Class<T> type, Function<T,String> converter)
    Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      public boolean isCleanStackTrace()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates if stack trace references to this library should be removed.
      Specified by:
      isCleanStackTrace in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      true by default
      See Also:

      public Map<String,Object> getContext()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Returns an unmodifiable map to append to the exception message.

      getContext in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      an empty map by default

    • withContext

      public GuavaRequirements withContext(String name, Object value)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message. Overrides any values associated with the name at the ThreadRequirements level.
      Specified by:
      withContext in interface Configuration
      Specified by:
      withContext in interface GuavaRequirements
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      value - the value of the parameter
      Returns:
      this

      Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.

      Parameters:

      Removes all contextual information associated with the exception message.

      Returns:

    • toString

      public String toString(Object value)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Returns the String representation of an object. By default, custom handlers are provided for arrays, Integer, Long, BigDecimal, and Path.
      Specified by:
      toString in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      value - a value
      Returns:
      the String representation of the value
      See Also:
      • Configuration.withStringConverter(Class, Function)

    • withStringConverter

      public <T> GuavaRequirements withStringConverter(Class<T> type, Function<T,String> converter)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      Please note that type must be an exact match (e.g. configuring a converter for Set will not match values of type HashSet).

      Specified by:
      withStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Specified by:
      withStringConverter in interface GuavaRequirements
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted (non-primitive arrays are mapped to Object[].class)
      converter - a function that converts an object of the specified type to a String
      Returns:
      this

    • withoutStringConverter

      public <T> GuavaRequirements withoutStringConverter(Class<T> type)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
      Specified by:
      withoutStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Specified by:
      withoutStringConverter in interface GuavaRequirements
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted
      Returns:
      this

    • requireThat

      public <K, V> MultimapVerifier<K,V> requireThat(Multimap<K,V> actual, String name)
      Description copied from interface: GuavaRequirements
      Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
      Specified by:
      requireThat in interface GuavaRequirements
      Type Parameters:
      K - the type of key in the multimap
      V - the type of value in the multimap
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value

    • assertThat

      public <K, V> MultimapVerifier<K,V> assertThat(Multimap<K,V> actual, String name)
      Description copied from interface: GuavaRequirements
      Same as GuavaRequirements.requireThat(Multimap, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
